title.en: Man-eating Beast that Devours the Righteous

summary.zh: 邽山上状如牛、毛如刺猬的食人恶兽；后世与浑敦、梼杌、饕餮并称四凶，传说它专食忠直、豢养奸恶。
summary.en: A man-eating beast of Mount Gui, ox-like with porcupine bristles; later counted among the Four Fiends, said to devour the loyal and reward the wicked.

description.zh:
《山海经·西次四经》记邽山之上有兽，“其状如牛，猬毛，名曰穷奇，音如獋狗，是食人”——形如牛而浑身猬刺，叫声像狗嗥。《海内北经》里它换了一副模样：状如虎，生双翼，吃人先从头开始，被吃的多是披发之人。

穷奇很早就进入了政治神话。《左传·文公十八年》说少皞氏有个不才子，毁信废忠、崇饰恶言，天下之民谓之穷奇；《史记·五帝本纪》记舜把浑敦、穷奇、梼杌、饕餮四凶族一并流放到四方边裔去抵御魑魅。《神异经》把它的性情写得更加刻薄：这兽能听懂人话，遇到有人争斗就吃掉有理的一方，听说谁忠信就咬掉他的鼻子，听说谁恶逆不善反倒捕来野兽相赠。

吊诡的是，汉代宫廷大傩的十二神兽里也有穷奇，《后汉书·礼仪志》所载傩仪咒辞命“穷奇腾根共食蛊”——这头颠倒是非的恶兽，在驱疫仪式上被派去吞食蛊毒。
description.en:
The 'Classic of the Western Mountains' places Qiongqi on Mount Gui: a beast shaped like an ox, bristled like a hedgehog, howling like a dog, and an eater of men. The 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: North' gives it another body — a winged tiger that devours people head first, favoring those who wear their hair loose.

Qiongqi entered political myth early. The Zuozhuan tells of a worthless son of the Shaohao clan who wrecked trust and dressed up slander, whom the people of the world named Qiongqi; in the Shiji, the sage-king Shun banishes him to the frontiers together with Hundun, Taowu, and Taotie — the Four Fiends. The Shenyijing sharpens the malice: the beast understands human speech, eats the party in the right whenever it finds a quarrel, bites the nose off the loyal and honest, and rewards the wicked with game it has caught.

Yet in the great exorcism of the Han court Qiongqi served the other side: the Book of the Later Han lists it among the twelve spirit-beasts of the Nuo rite, charged together with Tenggen to devour gu-poison.

value.zh: 状如牛，猬毛；一说如虎有翼
value.en: An ox with hedgehog bristles; elsewhere a winged tiger
Mount Gui, fourth guideway of the Western Mountains

sources.en: Shanhaijing, 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: North'
Book of the Later Han, 'Treatise on Rites'
